媒介 科研 随想 健身
2026年4月7日 ·
optimizationconvergence

Week 15: Convergence Analysis Progress

This week made decent progress on the convergence rate proof for the adaptive gradient method.

What I did

  • Filled in the gap in Lemma 3.2 — the key was bounding the variance term using the smoothness constant LLL more carefully.
  • Read [Defazio et al. 2023] on schedule-free optimizers; their variance reduction trick might be applicable.
  • Met with advisor Thursday, got feedback to tighten the assumptions in Section 4.

The key inequality

The main bound now looks like:

E[∥∇f(xt)∥2]≤2(f(x0)−f∗)T+σ2LT\mathbb{E}\left[\|\nabla f(x_t)\|^2\right] \leq \frac{2(f(x_0) - f^*)}{T} + \frac{\sigma^2 L}{T}E[∥∇f(xt​)∥2]≤T2(f(x0​)−f∗)​+Tσ2L​

where σ2\sigma^2σ2 is the gradient noise variance. Still need to handle the non-convex case.

Next week

  • Write up the proof cleanly
  • Start experiments on synthetic quadratic problems
  • Read [Khaled & Richtárik 2020] for comparison baselines
